chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI C.P.No.D-4632 of 2020 --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE(S) OF JUDGE(S) ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- For hearing of main case. 17.02.2025 Mr.Anwar Hussain, Advocate for the Petitioner. Ms.Alizeh Bashir, Assistant Attorney General. Present petition essentially impugns the two letters issued by Deputy Military Estates Officer, Karachi Circle, Karachi. The first one is dated 20.03.2019 and reads as follows:- “SUBJECT: MUTATION TRANSFER IN RESPECT OF BUNGALOW ON PLOT NO.83/II, 31ST STREET, PHASE-V-EXT, MEASURING 497 SQ.YARDS SITUATED IN DHA CLIFTON CANTONMENT KARACHI. Reference your application dated Nil. In order to proceed further in the subject matter, please provide Guardianship issued by the competent Court of Law in favour of Mr.Irfan Zakaria s/o Mr.Zakaria Ahmed.” The second letter dated 16.03.2020 reads as follows:- “SUBJECT: MUTATION TRANSFER IN RESPECT OF BUNGALOW ON PLOT NO.83/II, 31ST STREET, PHASE-V-EXT, MEASURING 497 SQ.YARDS SITUATED IN DHA CLIFTON CANTONMENT KARACHI. Reference your application dated Nil. Please be informed that your case was referred to the competent authority who advised the following:- “In the light of principles held in judgments, the alienation of minor’s property by father and transfer of such property without permission of the Court is not sustainable in law.” Nothing exceptional has been demonstrated to entertain this petition in writ jurisdiction. Learned counsel for the petitioner remains unable to articulate as to why the letters could not be responded.

Please be informed that your case was referred to the competent authority who advised the following:- “In the light of principles held in judgments, the alienation of minor’s property by father and transfer of such property without permission of the Court is not sustainable in law.” Nothing exceptional has been demonstrated to entertain this petition in writ jurisdiction. Learned counsel for the petitioner remains unable to articulate as to why the letters could not be responded. In view hereof, this petition being misconceived is dismissed. Judge Judge NASIR
